content.problemStatement:
Chef has two non negative integers NNN and XXX. He now wants to find the number of integers KKK such that 0≤K<N0 \le K \lt N0≤K<N, and (N⊕K)&X=0(N \oplus K) \And X = 0(N⊕K)&X=0.

Note that ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operator and &\And& denotes the bitwise AND operator.

Help Chef in finding the total required count.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing NNN and XXX denoting the two integers as described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output the total number of integers KKK which satisfy the requirements mentioned.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated non‑negative integers N and X (0 ≤ N, X ≤ 2^30‑1).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of integers K such that 0 ≤ K < N and ((N xor K) & X) = 0.

solution.approach: For a bit i where X has 1, the condition forces K_i = N_i. For bits where X has 0, K_i can be 0 or 1. Let base = N & X (the forced part). Any valid K can be written as K = base + V where V uses only positions with X_i = 0 (i.e., V & X = 0). The inequality K < N becomes V < N - base. Hence the problem reduces to counting numbers V in [0, limit‑1] (limit = N - base) that do not set any bit where X has 1. This is a classic digit‑DP on binary representation: iterate bits from most significant to least, keep a flag whether the prefix of V is already smaller than the prefix of limit (tight flag). At each bit, if X_i = 1 we can only place 0, otherwise we may place 0 or 1 respecting the tight condition. The DP runs in O(number of bits) ≈ 31 per test case, easily fast for T = 10^5.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * B) where B = 31 (number of bits needed for values up to 2^30).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
